questions.q: What is dissolution of a partnership?
questions.answer: Dissolution of a partnership is the process of bringing partnership to an end. It can be either voluntary or involuntary.

questions.q: What is the difference between dissolution and liquidation?
questions.answer: Dissolution is the process of bringing partnership to an end, while liquidation is the process of realizing the assets and discharging the liabilities of the firm.

questions.q: Who is responsible for the settlement of the accounts of the firm after its dissolution?
questions.answer: The partners are responsible for the settlement of the accounts of the firm after its dissolution.

questions.q: What is meant by a 'goodwill' on dissolution of a partnership?
questions.answer: Goodwill on dissolution of a partnership means the excess of the value of the firm over its net assets.

questions.q: What is the order of payment of assets on dissolution of a firm?
questions.answer: The order of payment of assets on dissolution of a firm is preferential claims, partners' capital, and partners' current accounts.

questions.q: What is meant by the 'order of priority' in the payment of assets on dissolution of a firm?
questions.answer: The order of priority in the payment of assets on dissolution of a firm means the payment of the debts of the firm in the order of preference.
questions.topic: Order of Priority in Payment of Assets

questions.q: What is the difference between 'goodwill' and 'goodwill on dissolution'?
questions.answer: Goodwill is the excess of the value of the firm over its net asset value, whereas goodwill on dissolution is the excess of the value of the firm over its net assets at the time of dissolution.

questions.q: What is meant by 'loss on dissolution'?
questions.answer: Loss on dissolution is the excess of the net liabilities of the firm over its assets at the time of dissolution.

questions.q: What is the procedure for settlement of accounts on dissolution of a firm?
questions.answer: The procedure for settlement of accounts on dissolution of a firm involves the following steps: (i) preparation of a balance sheet, (ii) determination of the amount to be brought in or paid out by the partners, (iii) preparation of a statement showing the share of each partner in the profits or losses, and (iv) payment of the amount to the partners.
key_points: Amount to be brought in or paid out
Statement of share of each partner

questions.q: Explain the method of calculating goodwill on dissolution of a partnership firm.
questions.answer: Goodwill on dissolution of a partnership firm is calculated as follows: (i) calculate the value of the firm, (ii) calculate the value of the net assets, (iii) determine the excess of the value of the firm over the value of the net assets, and (iv) multiply the excess by the agreed percentage.

questions.q: What is the effect of loss on dissolution on the partners' capital accounts?
questions.answer: Loss on dissolution is debited to the partners' capital accounts in the ratio of their capitals.

questions.q: Explain the process of settlement of accounts on dissolution of a partnership firm when the assets are not sufficient to pay the liabilities and the partners' capital.
questions.answer: When the assets are not sufficient to pay the liabilities and the partners' capital, the process of settlement of accounts involves the following steps: (i) payment of the liabilities in full, (ii) payment of the partners' capital in full, and (iii) distribution of the remaining assets among the partners in the ratio of their capitals.

questions.q: What is the effect of goodwill on dissolution on the partners' capital accounts?
questions.answer: Goodwill on dissolution is credited to the partners' capital accounts in the ratio of their capitals.

questions.q: Explain the method of calculating loss on dissolution of a partnership firm.
questions.answer: Loss on dissolution of a partnership firm is calculated as follows: (i) calculate the net liabilities of the firm, (ii) calculate the value of the net assets, (iii) determine the excess of the net liabilities over the value of the net assets, and (iv) multiply the excess by the agreed percentage.

questions.q: What is the effect of a firm's dissolution on the partners' liability?
questions.answer: The partners' liability is not affected by the dissolution of the firm. The partners are still liable for the firm's debts and obligations.
questions.topic: Effect of Dissolution on Partners' Liability

questions.q: Explain the procedure for distribution of the remaining assets among the partners in the ratio of their capitals.
questions.answer: The procedure for distribution of the remaining assets among the partners in the ratio of their capitals involves the following steps: (i) calculate the ratio of the partners' capitals, (ii) calculate the share of each partner in the remaining assets, and (iii) distribute the remaining assets among the partners in the ratio of their capitals.
